revision.comment: Use standard caps in section headers.
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[brackets]] or [[underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * {{DEFAULTSORT:''sortkey''}} (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: mass importing wikipedia pages
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[brackets]] or [[underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * {{DEFAULTSORT:''sortkey''}} (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[brackets]] or [[underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|Brackets]] or [[underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|Brackets]] or [[underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|Brackets]] or [[:en:Underscores|Underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[:en:Underscores|Underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Wikipedia features]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24543431184|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{For|more detailed information on magic words|Help:Magic words}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:MagicBegin|WP:MfB}} '''Magic words''' are words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|Brackets]] or [[:en:Underscores|Underscores]] which most often insert or display the current value of what they are called. These include '''parser functions''', '''variables''' and '''behavior switches''' and are features of [[WP:wikitext|wikitext]]. They are interpreted by the Metawiki software and are often used in templates so that they show different information depending on which page they are on and many other conditions. ==Types== There are three types of magic words: * '''Behavior switches:''' uppercase words surrounded by double [[:en:Underscores|Underscores]], like. __NOTOC__ * '''Variables:''' uppercase words surrounded by [[:en:Brackets|double braces]], like {{PAGENAME}} and function somewhat similarly to templates. * '''Parser functions:''' These include expressions and consist of keywords (some beginning #) in double braces with parameters following a colon, ex. {{#expr:2+2}} ==Things to remember== [[File:Nuvola apps knotes.png|thumb|270px|alt=A Notepad|Remember...]] * Most magic words may be case-sensitive. :*
''"{{SITENAME}}"'' produces "{{SITENAME}}", while ''"{{sitename}}"'' produces
{{sitename}} * Whitespace is not counted in from the front and back of keywords and parameters and the same also applies to template code. :*''"{{ Nofeed |Art=Wikipedia }}" and {{Nofeed|Art=Wikipedia}} will both succeed in producing the "Nofeed" template.'' * Page-dependent magic words will change or show information about the current page, even if the word is from a template, though in most cases, this is the desired effect. :*''If I insert {{exampletemplate}} and it says {{PAGENAME}}, I will see "Magic words for beginners" even though it is from "Template:example template".'' * If you want a magic word to paste in its value at the time of saving, you can [[Help:Substitution|substitute]] it in the same way that templates are substituted (using the subst: keyword). :*''{{CURRENTTIME}} will always display the current time and remain as is, while {{subst:CURRENTTIME}} will result in, for example, 9:45 being inserted and always remaining as regular text.'' ==Behavior switches== [[File:Magic wand.svg|thumb|240px|alt=A Magic Wand|The implementation of magic words]] ''For a more detailed explanation, refer to the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Behavior switches|behavior switches]] MediaWiki page.'' ===Table of contents=== * __NOTOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; prevents a table of contents) * __FORCETOC__ (can be placed anywhere in the wikitext; makes a table of contents appear in its normal position) * __TOC__ (places a table of contents at its position) ====Edit/section links==== * __NOEDITSECTION__ (hides section edit links beside headings) * __NEWSECTIONLINK__ (adds a "+" or "new section" link for adding a new section on a non-talk page) * __NONEWSECTIONLINK__ (removes the "+" or "new section" link on "Talk" pages) ====Categories and indexing==== * __NOGALLERY__ (on category pages, replaces thumbnails with normal links) * __HIDDENCAT__ (on category pages, makes it a [[WP:HIDDENCAT|hidden category]]) * __INDEX__ (tells search engines to index the page (show in results)) * __NOINDEX__ (tells search engines not to index the page (not show in results)) ====Titles and sorting==== * {{DISPLAYTITLE:''title''}} (changes the [[WP:DISPLAYTITLE|displayed form]] of the page title) * (sets a default category [[Help:Category#Sorting category pages|sorting key]]) ==Variables== [[File:Nuvola apps edu mathematics blue-p.svg|thumb|200px|alt=The Square Root of x|A variable in math]] [[File:Icon Mathematical Plot.svg|thumb|200px|alt=A mathematical plot.|A plot of variables in algebra.
(Though magic words are much easier!)]] ''For documentation, you may wish to read the [[mw:Help:Magic words#Variables|variables]] MediaWiki page.'' * {{FULLPAGENAME}} (The [[Help:Page name|page title]], including [[help:namespace|namespace]].) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAME}} (Ex. '''User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{PAGENAME}} (page title without namespace) :*''Shown here: {{PAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus/Adoption''')'' * {{BASEPAGENAME}} (the page title without the current [[Help:Subpages|subpage]] or namespace — the parent page without the namespace.) :*''Shown here: {{BASEPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:'''QwerpQwertus'''/Adoption)'' * {{SUBPAGENAME}} (subpage part of title) :*''Shown here: {{SUBPAGENAME}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/'''Adoption''')'' * {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (associated non-talk page) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Talk:Wikipedia" makes "Wikipedia")'' * {{TALKPAGENAME}} (associated talk page) :*''Shown here: {{TALKPAGENAME}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{NAMESPACE}} (namespace of current page) :*''Shown here: {{NAMESPACE}} (Ex. User:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (associated non-talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{SUBJECTSPACE}}, {{ARTICLESPACE}} (Ex. User talk:QwerpQwertus/Adoption makes "'''User'''")'' * {{TALKSPACE}} (associated talk namespace) :*''Shown here: {{TALKSPACE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''Talk:Wikipedia'''")'' * {{FULLPAGENAMEE}}, {{NAMESPACEE}} etc. (URL-encoded equivalents) :*''Shown here: {{FULLPAGENAMEE}} (Ex. "Wikipedia" makes "'''http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia'''")'' ==See also== For more detailed information on all magic words (behaviour switches, variables and parser functions), consider reading: * [[Help:Magic words]]: a more detailed help page. * [[mw:Help:Magic words]]: details of all available [[:en:MediaWiki|MediaWiki]] standard magic words. * [[mw:Help:Extension:ParserFunctions]]: parser function extensions to MediaWiki, to supplement magic words. * [[mw:Manual:Extending wiki markup]]: extending MediaWiki wiki markup by adding to standard tokens. {{Help navigation}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}}
